title: colobanthus squarrosus subsp. drucei
intro: Colobanthus squarrosus subsp. drucei, also known as Antarctic pearlwort, is a flowering plant that belongs to the family Caryophyllaceae. It is native to the sub-Antarctic islands and has been used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The plant is said to have health benefits such as reducing inflammation and improving wound healing due to its high content of antioxidants and other bioactive compounds. Herbal preparations of Colobanthus squarrosus subsp. drucei can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, although more research is needed to confirm its effectiveness and safety for human consumption.
